executive_summary: The Telegram channel @rt_english is assessed with high confidence as an official Russian state media channel operated by RT (formerly Russia Today), serving as the primary English-language Telegram distribution node for RT content. The channel mirrors RT editorial output with near-zero delay and functions as a direct extension of the state media apparatus on a platform with minimal content moderation.

key_findings.finding: Channel operates as direct broadcast extension of RT editorial pipeline, with 97% of messages being either original RT articles or forwards from sister RT channels, published within 3 minutes of website publication.

evidence.reference: Content analysis: 1,843 of 1,900 messages (97%) are RT-originated content during analysis period
Channel description explicitly states 'RT is a global news network' with link to rt.com
312 messages forwarded from @rt_russian within average 2.1 minutes of original posting

key_findings.finding: Channel serves as hub for a forwarding network of 15+ downstream channels that systematically amplify RT content to niche audiences, with forwarding occurring within 5 minutes for 89% of content.

evidence.reference: 15 channels identified forwarding from @rt_english with >50 forwards each during analysis period; top 5 channels forward >90% of RT English content
89% of forwarding events occur within 5 minutes of @rt_english posting, suggesting automated or semi-automated amplification

key_findings.finding: Content consistently promotes four documented Russian state narrative categories, with anti-NATO messaging appearing in 62% of analyzed messages and Ukraine conflict revisionism in 48%.

evidence.reference: Narrative coding of 1,900 messages: anti-NATO (62%, dominant), Ukraine revisionism (48%, frequent), Western hypocrisy (35%, frequent), anti-EU (28%, frequent)
Narrative alignment verified against EUvsDisinfo database: 23 matching entries for anti_nato_001, ukraine_003, west_hyp_001

key_findings.finding: Subscriber growth shows anomalous spikes of 8,000-12,000 new subscribers on dates coinciding with major geopolitical events, suggesting coordinated promotion or artificial inflation.

evidence.reference: Subscriber count grew from 485,000 to 524,000 during analysis period; three spikes of 8K-12K in single days (Feb 2, Feb 18, Mar 1)
Spike dates correlate with RT cross-promotion campaigns on Twitter and Facebook; organic growth baseline is ~200/day

subject_profile.account_bio: RT is a global news network. Watch RT LIVE at rt.com. Follow us for breaking news and commentary.

primary_narratives.narrative: NATO expansion threatens Russia's legitimate security interests and destabilizes Europe
Western intervention in Ukraine caused the conflict; Russia is responding defensively
Western media presents a biased, one-sided view; RT provides necessary alternative perspective
EU sanctions are self-destructive and harm European citizens more than Russia

behavioral_indicators.description: Average 35 messages per day, concentrated during 06:00-20:00 UTC with peak at 12:00 UTC; consistent 7 days/week with no weekend reduction
16.4% of messages (312/1900) forwarded from other RT channels (@rt_russian, @rt_arabic); 83.6% original posts. Forwarding delay averages 2.1 minutes from source.
Net growth of 39,000 subscribers over 52 days (750/day average), with three anomalous spikes of 8K-12K; organic baseline estimated at ~200/day
Average 85,000 views per message; 16.2% view-to-subscriber ratio; top posts reach 250K+ views indicating significant external sharing
Posting timestamps correlate with RT.com article publication times at r=0.91; cross-channel forwarding within RT network occurs within median 2.1 minutes

network_context.amplification_patterns: Content forwarded by 15+ downstream channels; top amplifiers are @geopolitics_uncensored (12K subs), @truth_global_news (8K subs), @multipolarity_now (5K subs)

source_attribution.content_origin: RT editorial team via rt.com publication pipeline; supplemented by forwards from sister RT language channels
source_attribution.amplification_chain: RT Editorial → rt.com → @rt_english (Telegram) → proxy channels → organic audiences

cross_platform_identities.linking_evidence: Same organization (RT); channel bio links to rt.com; content overlap >95%; cross-referenced in RT corporate communications

cannot_determine: Channel admin identities (Telegram hides admin lists by default)
Whether subscriber growth spikes are organic promotion-driven or artificially inflated
data_gaps: Channel creation date not exposed by Telegram API (estimated from earliest available message)
Subscriber demographics and geographic distribution unavailable
Deleted messages not recoverable; estimated 2-3% message gap based on ID sequence analysis
alternative_interpretations: High forwarding velocity could reflect a large editorial team with fast publication processes rather than automation
Subscriber growth spikes may correlate with legitimate news interest during geopolitical crises
assessment_changing_factors: Evidence of editorial independence from Russian state directives
Significant original reporting not sourced from Kremlin talking points
Channel admin identification revealing non-state actors

methodology.data_collection: Telegram MTProto API via Telethon — full message history pull for public channel
methodology.validation: Cross-referenced with EUvsDisinfo database (23 matching entries), GEC Pillars report classification, forwarding chain verified via MTProto forward metadata

evidence_references.reference: Telegram channel @rt_english — 524K subscribers, channel type, description text

evidence_references.notes: Coded by Gemini 1.5 Pro with v1.3 prompt
Forward metadata from Telegram message objects

evidence_references.reference: 15 downstream channels forwarding RT English content
